title: Well-posedness of the TECT gradient flow and minimisation problem
statement: Under (H1) mu^2>0 and (H2) gamma>0, the SCALAR Brazovskii L2 gradient flow $\partial_t\phi=-K(-i\nabla)\phi-\lambda\phi^3-\gamma\phi^5$ on a FIXED periodic cell $\mathbb{T}^3$ (K(q)=mu^2+Y(q^2-q_0^2)^2, A1-KERNEL-CONV) is globally well-posed for phi_0 in H^s, 3/2<s<=2: a unique global solution exists, is C^inf for t>0, depends continuously on phi_0, and F_TECT[phi(t)] is non-increasing. Standard analytic-semigroup theory (Henry/Pazy/Lunardi).
scope: NARROW (operator review 2026-06-23): ONE real scalar field, FIXED periodic cell, 3/2<s<=2. The spectral fact used is the lower bound lambda0:=min_k K(k) >= mu^2 > 0 (equality non-generic). Sobolev direction: for beta<=1/2, H^2 subset H^{4beta}=X^beta, so the energy H^2 a priori bound controls X^beta -> global continuation. s>2 persistence needs a separate higher-order estimate (NOT claimed). mu^2>0 is the DISORDERED-side condition giving a positive linear gap -- NOT a proof of BCC condensation. The full Class-II multi-field action (A2_full-ClassII) and the mu^2<0 condensate branch (A2_{mu^2<0}) are separate proof targets. Kernel hypothesis Y>0 (4th-order ellipticity / q^-4 decay) is made explicit in the consolidation v1.1.
falsifier: Finite-time blow-up or non-uniqueness for some phi_0 in H^s (3/2<s<=2) at mu^2>0, gamma>0; or lambda0:=min_k K(k) <= 0 (loss of sectoriality).

reproduction.expected: 8/8 PASS (exit 0); spectral inf=mu^2, Sobolev s>3/2/beta>3/8, smoothing C_beta=beta^beta e^-beta, Young C_*=1.01e-2, continuation window (3/8,1/2]

no_overclaim: s>2 persistence; the full Class-II multi-field action; the mu^2<0 condensate branch; any claim that mu^2>0 proves BCC condensation (it is the disordered-side linear gap); a unique global MINIMISER (Sector B); well-posedness for gamma<=0.
next_action: Operator sign-off of the narrowed T6; then A3-UV-SUPERRENORMALISABILITY (continuum limit); separately A2_full-ClassII and A2_{mu^2<0}.
notes: T6 PROVED CONDITIONAL (scalar/periodic/mu^2>0/gamma^2>0 scope, 3/2<s<=2) APPROVED by operator 2026-06-23 (T1->T6 PROVED CONDITIONAL) after review corrections: (1) lambda0>=mu^2>0 lower bound (not equality); (2) Sobolev direction H^2 subset X^beta; (3) s-range restricted to (3/2,2]; physical scope narrowed. Note v1.2 (v1.0,v1.1 superseded): adds the energy-domain global-existence patch (a priori bound via parabolic smoothing at t_0>0; F monotone for t>0, for t>=0 if phi_0 in H^2). Foundation for Sector-B free-energy minimisation. Next: A3. Reproduction bundle (T6, referee-grade, MANIFEST sha256): claims/A2-PDE-WELLPOSED/bundle/A2-WellPosedness-260623. Consolidation v1.1 (paper-grade): Y>0 explicit; constants written generally (C_*(lambda,gamma), C(mu^2,Y,q0)) with numerics as production-anchor checks. Re-linked 2026-06-23 to A1-SCALAR-ANALYTIC-BRANCH: the positivity hypothesis is the SHELL mass m_sh^2>0 (= old mu^2 = K(q_star)), NOT the zero-momentum r=K(0) nor the solver template's mu2=r. The current Class-II/condensate solver config is a mock (A1-PRODUCTION-KERNEL-MANIFEST fails) and is NOT cited as this theorem's numerical implementation.
